question: Will Bitcoin hit $150k by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for Bitcoin (BTC/USDT) has a final "High" price equal to or greater than the price specified in the title by 11:59PM ET on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "High" prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

events.title: When will Bitcoin hit $150k?
events.description: When will Bitcoin hit $150k

question: Iran closes its airspace by May 8?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran initiates a major closure of its airspace, that is not solely due to weather conditions, by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A “major closure” is defined as a broad closure, cancellation, or suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from Iranian airspace or a major Iranian Airspace region. A qualifying closure must apply generally to flights across Iran or a major Iranian airspace region. Limited cancellations, delays, or other partial closures will not qualify. Limited exceptions to a broad closure, however, will not disqualify such a closure from counting (e.g. exceptions for certain flights pre-approved by the Iranian Civil Aviation Authority may be permitted). 

Any non-weather complete suspension of commercial arrivals and departures affecting at least two of the following airports will qualify: Imam Khomeini International Airport (IKA), Mehrabad Airport (THR), Mashhad International Airport (MHD), Shiraz International Airport (SYZ), or Isfahan International Airport (IFN).

Qualifying previous examples include the January 2026 total closure of Iranian airspace except to international flights with express governmental permission (https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/iran-shuts-airspace-most-flights-flightradar24-says-2026-01-14/) and the April 2024 closure of Iran’s western airspace (https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/iranian-airports-cancel-flights-until-monday-morning-2024-04-14/). Non-qualifying examples include the January 27, 2026 partial closure of Iranian airspace around the Strait of Hormuz (https://www.aa.com.tr/en/middle-east/iran-issues-airspace-notice-for-military-drills-near-strait-of-hormuz/3812508) and the January 25 - April 25, 2026 VFR (Visual Flight Rules) flights suspension which did not broadly close commercial aviation in a qualifying region (https://www.intellinews.com/iran-suspends-general-aviation-and-vfr-flights-in-new-airspace-directive-421997/).

Warnings, No-Fly-Zones, or other flight restrictions imposed by airlines or countries other than Iran will not be sufficient for a “Yes” resolution.

Airspace closures which occur solely due to weather conditions will not qualify.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from Iranian aviation authorities and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid escalating US-Iran tensions following February strikes by US and Israeli forces that initially shuttered Iranian airspace, recent missile and drone launches from Iran's Hormozgan province toward UAE targets on May 5 prompted partial UAE airspace restrictions through May 11, fueling trader concerns over a renewed major closure of the Tehran FIR. Iran's Civil Aviation Authority partially reopened eastern corridors and select domestic routes in late April under a fragile ceasefire extended from early April, though international carriers continue avoiding the region due to IRGC drone shootdowns, contested air control, and US naval operations like Project Freedom. Upcoming China-mediated diplomacy involving Iranian Foreign Minister Araqchi and President Trump could ease risks, but Israeli warnings of retaliation keep probabilities elevated for broad commercial suspensions affecting key airports like IKA and THR.

question: Will the U.S. invade Iran before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Iran by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, land de facto controlled by Iran or the United States as of November 4, 2025 12:00 PM ET, will be considered the sovereign territory of that country.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible sources.

events.title: Will the U.S. invade Iran before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States commences a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Iran by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, land de facto controlled by Iran or the United States as of November 4, 2025 12:00 PM ET, will be considered the sovereign territory of that country.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible sources.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors no U.S. invasion of Iran before 2027 at 69.5%, reflecting de-escalation signals amid the ongoing 2026 Iran war, which began February 28 with U.S.-Israeli airstrikes on Iranian military and nuclear sites but has not escalated to ground operations. Iran's April 27 proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end hostilities, coupled with President Trump's review of a new diplomatic offer around May 2, has bolstered hopes for a negotiated settlement on nuclear issues and sanctions. Pentagon preparations for potential weeks-long ground actions remain options, not commitments, as recent congressional testimony emphasizes air defenses and drones over troop surges for invasion. Strait of Hormuz naval tensions persist, but upcoming Senate votes on authorizations for use of military force and budget requests could clarify escalation risks.

question: Lakers vs. Thunder
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for May 5 at 12:00AM ET:
If the Lakers win, the market will resolve to "Lakers".
If the Thunder win, the market will resolve to "Thunder".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Oklahoma City Thunder hold home-court advantage in Game 1 of the Western Conference Semifinals against the Los Angeles Lakers on May 5, hosting as the top seed after sweeping the season series 4-0 with dominant wins. Trader consensus reflects OKC's elite regular-season form led by Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, though Jalen Williams is out with a hamstring strain per the latest injury report. Lakers face major hurdles without Luka Doncic (hamstring), sidelining their star guard indefinitely and forcing reliance on LeBron James amid his ongoing foot management; Austin Reaves remains sidelined from earlier oblique issues. Recent playoff momentum favors Thunder's depth and rest advantage post-first round, while Lakers' path hinges on Doncic's timeline and road resilience in a best-of-seven.

question: Russia x Ukraine ceasefire by May 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, between Russia and Ukraine by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes," regardless of whether the ceasefire officially starts afterward.

Only ceasefires which constitute a general pause in the conflict will qualify. Ceasefires which only apply to energy infrastructure, the Black Sea, or other similar agreements will not qualify.

Any form of informal agreement will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A peace deal or political framework will qualify if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, effective on a specific date. Frameworks or agreements that outline terms for a future peace but do not include an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not count.

This market's resolution will be based on official announcements from both Russia and Ukraine; however, a wide consensus of credible media reporting stating an official ceasefire agreement between Russia and Ukraine has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a 94.5% chance against a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire by May 31, driven by stalled diplomatic efforts and persistent military escalation despite short-term tactical pauses. Russian President Vladimir Putin's recent proposal for a May 8-9 Victory Day truce—echoing the violated Orthodox Easter halt in April—has met Ukrainian counter-proposals for May 5-6, but both sides accuse each other of violations and issue threats, including Moscow's warning of massive missile strikes on Kyiv. U.S. President Trump's April 29 call with Putin discussed a possible pause, yet Zelenskyy seeks unclear details amid no new direct talks or concessions on core issues like territory and sanctions. Ongoing drone strikes, slowed Russian advances, and absent breakthroughs maintain high barriers to any general halt before the deadline.

question: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by May 15?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above 60 for any date between market creation and May 15, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for May 31, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 97.7% for Strait of Hormuz traffic returning to normal by May 15, reflecting severe ongoing disruptions from the 2026 US-Iran crisis, where shipping volumes remain at 15-20% of pre-crisis baselines—around 11 vessels daily versus the normal 60—following Iran's February 28 blockade and US "Project Freedom" naval escorts. Recent clashes, including US forces destroying Iranian boats and missile exchanges straining a fragile ceasefire as of May 4-5, have sustained the near-halt to commercial tanker transits, with over 2,000 ships stranded and war-risk premiums soaring. Full normalization faces barriers like electronic warfare, selective permitting, and mutual blockades, though a sudden diplomatic breakthrough or unilateral de-escalation could shift odds before the deadline.

question: Will the Iranian regime fall by May 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus gives a 97.2% implied probability that the Iranian regime will endure through May 31, reflecting its proven resilience after quelling the 2025–2026 protests by mid-January via widespread repression and massacres, despite subsequent economic strain from the US-led Strait of Hormuz blockade. Recent naval standoffs, including Iran's claim of repelling a US warship on May 4 and unverified accusations of attacks on UAE facilities, have heightened tensions but shown no signs of internal collapse, elite defections, or unified opposition capable of seizing power. Ongoing executions of dissenters underscore security forces' loyalty, with the short 26-day window to resolution leaving little room for upheaval absent a major catalyst like leadership vacuum or military breakdown.

question: Will Morocco win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the 2026 FIFA World Cup groups locked in after late-March play-offs securing spots for Sweden, Türkiye, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Czechia, Congo DR, and Iraq, trader consensus bunches top implied probabilities amid balanced paths to the Round of 32. France leads narrowly at 17% on Kylian Mbappé's golden boot favoritism and March friendlies triumphs over Brazil (2-1) and Colombia (3-1), bolstering attack depth in a Group I featuring Senegal and Norway. Spain (15%) draws strength from Lamine Yamal's emergence and a manageable Group H vs. Uruguay; England (11%) eyes Group L despite Croatia; Argentina and Brazil (both 9%) rely on Lionel Messi's magic and Vinícius Júnior's flair, underscoring a fiercely competitive 48-team field.

question: US x Iran permanent peace deal by May 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran and the United states agree to a permanent peace deal by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ongoing indirect US-Iran negotiations, mediated by Pakistan since the April 8, 2026, ceasefire in the Iran war, center on a permanent peace deal addressing nuclear restrictions, sanctions relief, ballistic missiles, and Strait of Hormuz navigation. Iran's recent 14-point proposal—offering phased nonaggression pacts and reopening the strait while delaying nuclear talks—drew President Trump's rejection as insufficient, highlighting sticking points after marathon sessions yielded no breakthrough. Diplomatic rhetoric persists amid US naval escorts and Iranian threats, with trader consensus reflecting cautious optimism for further Pakistan-hosted talks. Regional dynamics, including Israel and proxies, plus historical negotiation failures like the JCPOA collapse, underscore barriers to agreement by market deadlines.

question: Will Yaël Braun-Pivet win the 2027 French presidential election?
description:
The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held around April 2027.  This market pertains to the outcome of the next French presidential election, regardless of whether it follows the scheduled end of the current term or is held earlier. 

The President of France is elected via a two-round system; a candidate must secure over 50% of the vote to win outright in the first round. If no candidate achieves this, the top two contenders advance to a runoff.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If, for any reason, the results of the election are not known by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the French Government, specifically the Ministry of the Interior (https://www.interieur.gouv.fr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls after March municipal elections, where the far-right Rassemblement National underperformed in major cities, show Jordan Bardella leading first-round intentions at 30-38%, with Édouard Philippe surging to 20-25% as Horizons' standard-bearer and the only moderate seen beating him in a runoff, aligning with trader consensus pricing both near 20-24%. The race stays tight amid a crowded field, Macron's term limits barring reelection, and ongoing coalition instability from the 2024 snap legislative vote, fostering strategic "vote utile" dynamics across fragmented left and right blocs. Primaries like Les Républicains' selection of Bruno Retailleau, Jean-Luc Mélenchon's recent candidacy announcement, economic shocks, or gaffes could widen leads ahead of the April 2027 contest.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for May 31, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of May?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above 60 for any date between market creation and May 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for May 31, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ongoing U.S.-Iran naval clashes in the Strait of Hormuz, including U.S. forces sinking Iranian boats and intercepting missiles on May 4, have reinforced Iran's selective blockade on commercial shipping, keeping transits at 15-20% of pre-conflict norms of 100-140 vessels daily. Traffic plummeted over 95% since the late February war escalation and supreme leader assassination, with only sporadic U.S.-escorted passages amid a fragile April ceasefire now under strain. Washington's "Project Freedom" to guide stranded ships faces Iranian threats of further retaliation, stranding hundreds of vessels and spiking insurance premiums. Traders price an 84.5% chance against normalization by May 31 absent de-escalation or successful diplomacy.

question: Will Eric Trump win the 2028 Republican presidential nomination?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Republican Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican Party sources.

Any replacement of the Republican nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. holds a narrow trader consensus lead at 49% for the 2028 Republican presidential nomination, propelled by his recent congressional testimony alleging pharmaceutical industry bribery and vocal clashes with Democrats on vaccine policies, enhancing his anti-establishment appeal amid GOP base frustrations. Vice President J.D. Vance trails closely at 39.3%, his odds pressured by backlash over the ongoing Iran conflict, where his cautious stance on escalation contrasts with hawkish voices like Secretary of State Marco Rubio at 21.1%, who gains from foreign policy visibility. This tight race reflects uncertainty in an open primary post-Trump term limits, with 2026 midterms, early Iowa caucus polling, and Trump family endorsements poised to tip the balance among key voting blocs.

question: LoL: Dplus KIA vs Nongshim Red Force (BO3) - Esports World Cup Korea Qualifier Playoffs
description:
This market refers to the LoL Upper bracket quarterfinal 2 match between Dplus KIA and Nongshim Red Force in the Esports World Cup Korea Qualifier Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 4 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Dplus KIA" if Dplus KIA win the match against Nongshim Red Force.

This market will resolve to "Nongshim Red Force" if Nongshim Red Force win the match against Dplus KIA.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Dplus KIA holds a commanding 23-5 head-to-head edge over Nongshim RedForce across 28 prior BO3 series, with midlaner ShowMaker's macro dominance and botlane duo Smash-Career providing consistent carry threat, fueling trader consensus toward DK in this EWC Korea Qualifier upper bracket playoff. However, NS's stunning 2-0 upset over DK in LCK 2026 Rounds 1-2 highlights their aggressive style via top Kingen and veteran support Lehends, though a 2-0 sweep by KIWOOM DRX three days ago tempers momentum amid their 4-6 Split 2 record versus DK's 6-4 mark including recent T1 victories. Stable rosters and current patch favor macro skirmishes; winner advances to face Hanwha Life Esports.

question: Will Club Atlético de Madrid win on 2026-05-05?
description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 5, 2026
If Club Atlético de Madrid wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Arsenal FC vs. Club Atlético de Madrid
events.description: This event is for the upcoming UEFA Champions League game, scheduled for Tuesday, May 5, 2026 between Arsenal FC and Club Atlético de Madrid.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal enter the UEFA Champions League semi-final second leg at Emirates Stadium with a 61.5% implied probability of victory after a 1-1 first-leg draw in Madrid, bolstered by home advantage and a dominant 3-0 Premier League win over Fulham last weekend where Viktor Gyökeres scored twice. Key returns of Martin Ødegaard and Kai Havertz from knocks enhance their attacking depth, despite absences like Jurriën Timber and Mikel Merino, while Atlético Madrid contend without midfield anchors Pablo Barrios and Nico González due to thigh injuries, though Julián Álvarez is back in the squad post-first-leg knock. Traders price the draw at 22.5% reflecting the tie's tension and Atlético's resilient away form, but Arsenal's unbeaten UCL campaign and strong record versus Spanish sides in London underpin their favoritism over the 16.5% Atlético win chance.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices California Governor Gavin Newsom as the clear early frontrunner at 26% implied probability for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination, propelled by his consistent leads in recent national primary polls like Emerson College's surveys showing 20-25% support and a March Politico poll giving him a 14-point edge over Kamala Harris in California. Harris and Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez tie at 8%, buoyed by lingering name recognition and progressive appeal, respectively, while Senator Jon Ossoff and Pennsylvania Governor Josh Shapiro trail amid a fragmented field lacking an incumbent. Newsom differentiates via national fundraising prowess, anti-Trump media profile, and term-limited governorship freeing him for a run; consolidation could hinge on 2026 midterm outcomes boosting swing-state executives, donor endorsements, or party soul-searching after 2024.

question: Will the Minnesota Timberwolves win the 2026 NBA Finals?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Minnesota Timberwolves win the 2026 NBA Finals. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NBA Finals based off the rules of the NBA.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder's 59.5% implied probability reflects trader consensus on their dominance as defending champions and Western Conference top seed, highlighted by a commanding 4-0 first-round sweep over the Suns where Shai Gilgeous-Alexander erupted for 42 points in Game 4's 131-122 clincher, positioning them strongly for the semifinals against the Lakers amid optimal rest. San Antonio Spurs at 15.7% gained traction with their first playoff series win since 2017, ousting the Trail Blazers 5-? before Game 2 versus Minnesota Timberwolves on May 5. New York Knicks' 9.8% stems from a 137-98 Game 1 rout of upset-minded 76ers in East semis on May 4, boosting Jalen Brunson-led momentum past the Hawks. Detroit Pistons' 5.5% holds as East No. 1 despite a grueling 4-3 series over Magic, now facing Cavaliers in semifinals.

question: Russia x Ukraine ceasefire by June 30, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, between Russia and Ukraine by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes," regardless of whether the ceasefire officially starts afterward.

Only ceasefires which constitute a general pause in the conflict will qualify. Ceasefires which only apply to energy infrastructure, the Black Sea, or other similar agreements will not qualify.

Any form of informal agreement will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A peace deal or political framework will qualify if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, effective on a specific date. Frameworks or agreements that outline terms for a future peace but do not include an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not count.

This market's resolution will be based on official announcements from both Russia and Ukraine; however, a wide consensus of credible media reporting stating an official ceasefire agreement between Russia and Ukraine has been reached will suffice.

events.title: Russia x Ukraine ceasefire by June 30, 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, between Russia and Ukraine by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes," regardless of whether the ceasefire officially starts afterward.

Only ceasefires which constitute a general pause in the conflict will qualify. Ceasefires which only apply to energy infrastructure, the Black Sea, or other similar agreements will not qualify.

Any form of informal agreement will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A peace deal or political framework will qualify if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, effective on a specific date. Frameworks or agreements that outline terms for a future peace but do not include an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not count.

This market's resolution will be based on official announcements from both Russia and Ukraine; however, a wide consensus of credible media reporting stating an official ceasefire agreement between Russia and Ukraine has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent short-term unilateral ceasefires—Russia's for May 8-9 Victory Day and Ukraine's for the night of May 5-6—underscore the lack of bilateral agreement amid ongoing military actions, including Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian oil refineries in Perm and Novorossiysk, and Russian threats of massive missile retaliation. President Putin's proposal, floated in talks with U.S. President Trump last week, draws Ukrainian demands for details on a "long-term and guaranteed" truce, viewing it as symbolic for Moscow's parade rather than a diplomatic breakthrough. With maximalist territorial positions entrenched, no scheduled peace negotiations, and persistent frontline escalations despite slowed Russian advances, traders price an 89.5% "No" probability, reflecting formidable barriers to resolution by June 30 absent major de-escalation signals.

question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $76,000 on May 5?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.
